Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: We need to analyze the event log against the normative rules and desiderata. Lets list each case.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 1: sequence appears correct: App Received, Credit Check, Risk Assessment, Loan Decision (Approved), Inform Applicant, Finalize Documentation, Close Case. No violations.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Desiderata: Check timing: Credit check same day (30 min later) fine; Inform applicant same day (2 hours after decision) fine; Follow-up call not present? No mention of follow-up call, so desideratum violation. So only missing follow-up call.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 2: sequence: App Received (08:00), then Risk Assessment (08:20) before Credit Check (09:00) - violates normative order: Credit Check must occur before Risk Assessment.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Also after Loan Decision (Rejected), they did Finalize Documentation (10:00) which is not allowed after rejection. There is no Inform Applicant activity in the log. No Inform Applicant! That is a normative violation as well - after loan decision, must inform applicant. So multiple violations.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Case 3: sequence: App Received (Jan12 09:00), Credit Check (Jan14 10:00 - 49 hours later), Risk Assessment (Jan14 11:00), Loan Decision (Approved, Jan14 12:00), Inform Applicant (Jan16 15:00 - about 51 hours after decision? Actually Jan14 12:00 to Jan16 15:00 is 51 hours, slightly over 48), Finalize Documentation (Jan17 09:00), Close Case (Jan17 10:00). Check normative rules: Order is correct: Credit Check before Risk Assessment, etc. Loan approved, then inform, finalize, close. So no normative violations.

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Desiderata: Credit check should be within 24 hours after application; it was 49 hours - violation. Inform applicant within 48 hours of decision; it was 51 hours - violation. Also no follow-up call - violation. So three desiderata violations.

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Now answer the task. 1. Normative violations: Case 2 violates sequence (Risk Assessment before Credit Check), does not inform applicant, and finalizes documentation after rejection. No other cases violate normative rules. 2. Desiderata violations: Case 1: missing follow-up call (but timing ok). Case 2: missing follow-up call. Case 3: credit check >24h, inform >48h, missing follow-up call.

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: 3. Interpretations: For case 2, it is a clear process violation; perhaps due to error. For case 3, delays might be due to workload or weekends. Timing violations are mild. Missing follow-up call might be due to resource constraints.
